title: Connection lifecycle — age tracking, reconnection, and warm floor
purpose: Implement connection lifecycle management so that WS connections survive long sessions. Each slot tracks its connection age and proactively reconnects before the 60-minute server limit. Unexpected server Close frames trigger reconnection. The warm floor is established lazily on first pool access, not eagerly at provider construction.
task: Extend the pool's take path to check connection age before handing a connection to a caller. If age exceeds (60 min - reconnect_margin), close the connection, establish fresh, and hand the new one to the caller. Handle websocket_connection_limit_reached errors during a request by closing, re-establishing, and retrying once. Handle unexpected WS Close frames by marking the slot Disconnected. Establish warm floor connections on first pool access.

requirements.title: Per-slot age tracking
requirements.spec: Each WsSlot SHALL track established_at: Option<Instant> set when a WS connection is successfully established. The age SHALL be computed as Instant::now() - established_at.
acceptance: established_at set on successful WsConnection establishment
established_at is None for slots in Disconnected state

requirements.title: Proactive reconnection before server limit
requirements.spec: WHEN a caller takes an Available connection AND the connection age exceeds (60 min - reconnect_margin) THE SYSTEM SHALL close the old connection, establish a fresh one in the same slot, and hand the fresh connection to the caller.
acceptance: Connection older than (60 min - reconnect_margin) is closed before use
Fresh connection established in the same slot
Caller receives the fresh connection, not the stale one
established_at updated to reflect the new connection

requirements.title: 60-minute limit error handling
requirements.spec: IF the server sends websocket_connection_limit_reached during a request THE SYSTEM SHALL mark the connection broken, establish a fresh connection in the slot, and retry the request once on the new connection.
acceptance: websocket_connection_limit_reached error detected from WS error envelope
Old connection closed, fresh one established
Request retried once on the new connection
If retry also fails, error propagated to caller

requirements.title: Unexpected Close frame handling
requirements.spec: IF the server sends an unexpected WS Close frame THE SYSTEM SHALL mark the slot as Disconnected. The next caller to encounter the Disconnected slot SHALL establish a fresh connection.
acceptance: WS Close frame sets slot state to Disconnected
Next request on this slot triggers fresh establishment

requirements.title: Lazy warm floor establishment
requirements.spec: WHEN the pool is accessed for the first time THE SYSTEM SHALL establish the warm floor via parallel tokio::spawn tasks — one per warm slot. The first caller waits only for its own connection; the remaining warm floor connections establish in the background. The pool SHALL NOT establish connections eagerly at provider construction.
acceptance: No WS connections established during OpenAiProvider::new()
First call to the pool triggers warm floor establishment
First caller's connection established synchronously; remaining slots spawn as background tasks
Background warm floor tasks complete and set their slots to Available without blocking any caller

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the WsState enum or pool structure — use what PW-005 provides
SHALL NOT implement request framing — use PW-007 send/receive functions
SHALL NOT hardcode the 55-minute threshold — derive from the 60-minute limit with configurable margin
verification: Tests verify proactive reconnection at 55-minute age boundary
Tests verify websocket_connection_limit_reached triggers retry on fresh connection
Tests verify Close frame marks slot Disconnected
Tests verify warm floor established on first pool access, not at construction
